stories.title: Sabalenka vs. Gauff — WTA Miami Final, Today
stories.angle: The biggest WTA final of the year so far. H2H locked at 6-6 — 13th meeting breaks the tie. Sabalenka seeking Sunshine Double. Gauff playing at her "home" tournament in Florida.
stories.posting_window: 8:00 AM ET (preview) + 9:00 PM ET (result recap if available)

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: The WTA Miami Final is TODAY. Sabalenka (No. 1) vs. Gauff (No. 4). H2H tied 6-6 — this one breaks the tie. Sabalenka hasn't dropped a set all tournament. Gauff won her last two sets 12-2. 🎾 3:00 PM ET #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #WTA
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM ET (preview) + 9:00 PM ET (result recap if available)

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Sabalenka bids for the Sunshine Double. Gauff plays in front of a home crowd in Florida. H2H 6-6. It doesn't get bigger than this. 🏆 WTA Miami Final starting now on ESPN. #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #Sabalenka
x_posts.posting_time: 8:00 AM ET (preview) + 9:00 PM ET (result recap if available)

facebook.long_form:
```
Today's the day. The 2026 Miami Open women's final is here — and it's the rematch tennis has been waiting for.

No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka and No. 4 Coco Gauff meet for the 13th time today at Hard Rock Stadium, and the story could not be more compelling. Sabalenka has been immaculate through this event: six matches, zero sets dropped, a commanding semifinal win over Elena Rybakina, and the kind of aggressive baseline tennis that makes her look virtually unbeatable on a good day. She already won Indian Wells this season and is chasing the Sunshine Double — a rare double-header sweep that would cement her as the undisputed best player in the world right now.

Gauff's path here couldn't be more different. Four of her five matches before the final went to three sets — she fought, scraped, and clawed through the bottom half of the draw. Then came the semifinal, where she unleashed a completely different performance: a 6-1, 6-1 demolition of Karolína Muchová that took just 89 minutes. She's playing at home in Florida, has the crowd behind her, and has clearly found another gear at the right moment.

The H2H sits at exactly 6-6 heading in. There's no edge, no clear favorite — just two elite players who know each other's games inside out, going for the biggest title of their young season.

Who wins today's final — Sabalenka or Gauff? Drop your pick below.
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM ET

```
Today's the day. Sabalenka vs. Gauff. WTA Miami Final. H2H: 6-6.
```

stories.title: Sinner vs. Lehecka — Miami ATP Final Preview, Sunday
stories.angle: Sinner seeks the Sunshine Double — joins Federer 2017 if he wins. Lehecka seeking first Masters 1000 title, never broken serve in 5 SF matches. Generational clash.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Sunday's ATP Miami Final: Sinner vs. Lehecka. Sinner chases the Sunshine Double (last man: Federer, 2017). Lehecka hasn't had his serve broken ONCE in 5 Miami matches. One of these streaks ends Sunday. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #ATP

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: If Sinner wins Miami Sunday, he closes the ATP No. 1 gap to Alcaraz from 2,140 to 1,240 points. Alcaraz then defends 4,300 clay points this spring. The rankings race is very much alive. 📊 #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #ATPRankings

facebook.long_form:
```
Sunday's Miami Open final is a classic David-and-Goliath setup — and Goliath is trying to complete one of tennis's most coveted achievements.

Jannik Sinner (No. 2) arrives at the final having beaten Alexander Zverev 6-3, 7-6(4) on Friday night — 15 aces, zero double faults, and a clinical 7-4 tiebreak close when Zverev pushed him to the limit. Sinner already won Indian Wells this season, and a Miami title would make him the first man since Roger Federer in 2017 to complete the Sunshine Double. His dominance over the elite competition has been remarkable: he leads the H2H 8-4 against Zverev after seven consecutive wins.

On the other side, 21st-seeded Jiří Lehečka produced one of the most emphatic semifinal performances you'll see: a 6-2, 6-2 rout of Arthur Fils in just 75 minutes on Friday afternoon. But the more remarkable stat is this — Lehečka has not had his serve broken a single time across all five of his Miami matches. In a tournament where the world's best returners are competing, that kind of service dominance is extraordinary. The 24-year-old Czech is going for his first Masters 1000 title, following in the footsteps of his compatriot Jakub Menšík, who won this title a year ago.

Sunday's final at Hard Rock Stadium will decide who takes the Sunshine Double — and whether the unstoppable Sinner run continues, or Lehečka writes one of the tournament's great upset stories.

Who takes the Miami ATP title on Sunday — Sinner or Lehečka?
```

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM ET

```
Sunday: Sinner vs. Lehečka. ATP Miami Final. Can anyone stop Sinner's Sunshine Double bid?
```

stories.title: Sinner d. Zverev 6-3, 7-6(4) — Miami SF Recap
stories.angle: Sinner dominated, then had to dig deep. 15 aces. Zverev saved all 3 break points, forced a tiebreak — but Sinner closed it 7-4.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Sinner into the Miami final. Beat Zverev 6-3, 7-6(4). 15 aces. 0 double faults. Zverev forced a tiebreak — Sinner closed it 7-4. H2H now 8-4 Sinner. 7 straight wins. The man is untouchable. 🏆 #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #Sinner

facebook.long_form:
```
Jannik Sinner is in the Miami Open final, and he made it look easier than a 7-6 tiebreak might suggest.

Sinner beat Alexander Zverev 6-3, 7-6(4) on Friday night, serving at an elite level throughout: 15 aces, zero double faults, and 74% of first serves landing in. He broke Zverev's serve in the opening set and controlled most of the second — but the world No. 4 is no pushover. Zverev fought hard, saving all three break points he faced in the match and forcing a tiebreak. For a few minutes, it looked like the German might level the contest. But Sinner was clinical under pressure — he won the tiebreak 7-4 without drama.

What stands out is not just the result but the manner. This was Sinner's seventh straight win over Zverev — H2H now 8-4 — and every recent meeting has followed the same pattern: Zverev competes, but Sinner finds another level when it matters. Sunday's final against Lehečka will be a different kind of test, but you'd have a hard time betting against the world No. 2 right now.

Do you think Sinner completes the Sunshine Double on Sunday?
```

#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM ET

```

```

stories.title: Lehecka d. Fils 6-2, 6-2 — Miami SF Recap
stories.angle: Dominant 75-minute rout. Lehecka has not been broken ONCE in 5 Miami matches. Fils had nothing left after saving 4 MPs vs Tommy Paul.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Lehecka demolished Fils 6-2, 6-2 in 75 minutes. First Miami final. 5 matches without giving up a single service break. 82% first-serve points won yesterday. He's been the most dominant server in this draw. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #MiamiOpen #Lehecka

facebook.long_form:
```
Jiří Lehečka made a strong case for "most dominant player of the Miami draw" on Friday afternoon — and it wasn't even close.

The 24-year-old Czech beat Arthur Fils 6-2, 6-2 in 75 minutes to reach his first Masters 1000 final. The numbers tell the story: 6 aces, zero double faults, 82% of first-serve points won — and, crucially, not a single service break faced across the entire match. When you add that to his four previous Miami matches, Lehečka has not been broken once in this entire tournament. Five matches. Every service hold.

For Fils, it was a tough afternoon. The 21-year-old Frenchman had saved four match points against Tommy Paul two days earlier in one of the Miami Open's most memorable matches — but he had little gas left against Lehečka's relentless serving. The H2H is now level at 2-2 between them.

Lehečka becomes the 8th Czech player to reach a Masters 1000 final since the series began in 1990. He'll face Sinner on Sunday with a chance to join compatriot Jakub Menšík, who won Miami just a year ago. Czech tennis is having a moment.

What do you think of Lehečka's chances against Sinner Sunday?
```

```

```

stories.title: Goffin Retiring + Djokovic Skips Monte Carlo
stories.angle: Two significant news items in 24 hours: Goffin announces 2026 is his final season; Djokovic confirmed out of Monte Carlo with right shoulder.

x_posts.label: Post 5-1
x_posts.text: David Goffin announced 2026 will be his final season. Career No. 7 in the world. 6 ATP titles. First Belgian ever in the Top 10. A great career coming to a close. 👏 #TennisFanrecap #Goffin #ATP

x_posts.label: Post 5-2
x_posts.text: Djokovic is out of Monte Carlo (right shoulder). First time he's missed the tournament since 2011. Clay season is opening without him — and without Alcaraz, who exits Miami early. The draw is wide open. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #Djokovic #MonteCarlo

facebook.long_form:
```
Two significant off-court stories surfaced Friday, and both carry a bittersweet tone.

First: David Goffin announced via Instagram that 2026 will be his final season on the ATP Tour. The Belgian is 35 years old, has been battling a knee injury, and after 17+ years of professional tennis, has decided this is the right time to close the chapter. Goffin's career deserves more recognition than it often received — he reached No. 7 in the world, won six ATP titles, and made history as the first Belgian ever to break into the Top 10. He also knocked out both Federer and Nadal at the 2017 ATP Finals in London. Plans for after retirement include a personal documentary, coaching work, and — apparently — a marathon.

Second: Novak Djokovic has confirmed his withdrawal from the Monte Carlo Masters (April 5-12) due to his ongoing right shoulder injury. It will be Djokovic's first absence from Monte Carlo since 2011 — a stretch of 14 consecutive appearances now coming to an end. He skipped Indian Wells and Miami as well, meaning he'll go into the clay season rusty. Monte Carlo will proceed headlined by Alcaraz, Sinner, and Zverev, with Djokovic hoping to return at Madrid in late April.

Two of the sport's most decorated players navigating very different ends to their stories.

A tribute to Goffin's career — what's your favorite Goffin moment?
```
facebook.image_caption:
#### Image Caption -- 10:00 AM ET

```
Goffin retires at season's end. Djokovic misses Monte Carlo. A bittersweet Friday in tennis.
```
